Neoplasms (C00-D49).Description: Chapter 2 of the ICD-10-CM contains codes for most benign and all malignant neoplasms. Some benign neoplasms may appear in body system-specific chapters. Correct coding requires determining if the neoplasm is benign, in-situ, malignant, or of uncertain behavior. For malignant neoplasms, secondary (metastatic) sites should also be identified.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Anemia due to chemotherapy/immunotherapy: Anemia first, then neoplasm and adverse effect code (T45.1X5-).
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Anemia due to radiotherapy: Anemia first, then neoplasm and Y84.2.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Treatment of complication from surgical: List complication first if treatment is for it.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Primary malignancy previously excised: Use Z85 code for history; secondary site becomes principal if present.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Episode of care involves surgical remova: If surgery + adjunct therapy in same stay — neoplasm is principal diagnosis.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Patient admission chiefly for administra: Use Z51.0, Z51.11, or Z51.12 as principal; malignancy is secondary.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Complications during therapy: Therapy code is principal, followed by complication codes.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Admission/encounter to determine extent: Malignancy or metastatic site is principal diagnosis.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Symptoms, signs, and abnormal findings a: Cannot replace malignancy as principal diagnosis.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Malignancy in two or more noncontiguous: Clarify with provider if different primaries or metastases.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Disseminated malignant neoplasm, unspeci: Use C80.0 only if no primary or secondary sites are known.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Malignant neoplasm without specification: Use C80.1 only when primary site cannot be determined.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Complication associated with a neoplasm: Complication first, then neoplasm (except anemia — malignancy first, then D63.0).
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Complication from surgery: Complication first; determine if neoplasm code also applies.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Pathologic fracture due to neoplasm: If fracture treatment is focus, M84.5 first; if neoplasm is focus, neoplasm first.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Current malignancy versus personal histo: Primary malignancy code used until treatment completed; Z85 codes afterward.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Leukemia, Multiple Myeloma, and Malignan: Remission status must be documented; otherwise query provider.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Malignant neoplasm associated with trans: T86.- first, then C80.2, then malignancy code.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Breast Implant Associated Anaplastic Lar: Use C84.7A (active) or C84.7B (in remission); do not assign Chapter 19 complication code.
Neoplasms (C00-D49).Secondary malignant neoplasm of lymphoid: Code from C81–C85 with extranodal site final character instead of coding the solid organ.
